# Copilot code review — KSail

KSail is a **Go CLI + MCP/AI tool server** for declaratively provisioning local & cloud Kubernetes
clusters (Kind/K3d/Talos/vCluster/KWOK/EKS) and GitOps workloads. Canonical conventions live in
[`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md); deeper per-path rules live in
[`.github/instructions/`](instructions/) (`go-code`, `go-testing`, `docs`). This file is the concise
repo-wide review checklist — flag violations of the rules below.

## Always (every PR)

- **Conventional-Commit PR title** (`feat:`/`fix:`/`docs:`/`ci:`/`chore:`/`refactor:`/`test:`/`perf:`).
  Every PR squash-merges on its title → changelog/release, so a non-conventional or bracket-prefixed
  title is a defect.
- **Root-cause fixes only.** Flag anything that masks a failure instead of fixing it: `t.Skip` added to
  dodge a red test, `//nolint` / disabled linters, `continue-on-error` to hide CI, deleted or weakened
  assertions, `--no-verify`, a check called "flaky" without evidence.
- **Never hand-edit generated files — re-run the generator.** Generated here:
  `docs/src/content/docs/cli-flags/`, `docs/src/content/docs/configuration/declarative-configuration.mdx`,
  and `schemas/ksail-config.schema.json` (`go generate ./docs/...` / `go generate ./schemas/...`). A
  hand-edit to any of these, or a stale schema/flag doc not regenerated after a flag change, is a finding.
- **Behaviour / flag / command / config / UX change ⇒ its docs updated in the same PR** — the generated
  CLI-flags reference, the JSON schema, `AGENTS.md`, and the relevant devantler.tech page. A missing doc
  update is a review finding.
- **No secrets in plaintext** — flag committed tokens, keys, or kubeconfigs.
- PRs from the Daily AI Assistant carry a `> 🤖 Generated by the Daily AI Assistant` line — expected,
  not a finding.

## Go / KSail stack

- **Validation gate:** `go build -o /tmp/ksail .`, `go test ./...`, `golangci-lint run --timeout 5m`
  must all pass. Flag code that won't build, lint clean, or that drops/weakens a test.
- **Tests pin real behaviour** (see [`go-testing.instructions.md`](instructions/go-testing.instructions.md)):
  prefer table-driven tests; cover command and reconcile paths and error branches; never assert on
  nothing. Mocks are generated (`mockery`, in `mocks.go` files) — regenerate, don't hand-tweak.
- **AI tool registration is automatic** — runnable CLI commands are exposed as MCP/chat tools via
  `pkg/toolgen/` from the Cobra command tree (`ai.toolgen.consolidate` + `ai.toolgen.permission` →
  `cluster_read`/`cluster_write`/`workload_*`/`tenant_write`/`cipher_write`). Flag any PR that manually
  registers a tool handler instead of relying on toolgen, or adds a command without considering its
  auto-exposed read/write tool surface.
- **Benchmark gate consistency:** if a PR changes `-count` in the CI `go test -bench` step, the awk
  averaging/filtering step must still emit exactly one line per benchmark name — otherwise it false-flags
  regressions. Flag a `-count` change that doesn't update that step (see `docs/BENCHMARK-REGRESSION.md`).
- **Cluster safety in tests/maint:** only touch clusters the code created; build throwaway binaries to
  `/tmp`, never `./ksail`. Flag tests that assume a shared/pre-existing cluster.

## GitHub Actions / CI

- Pin third-party actions by SHA; keep workflows `actionlint`-clean. Bundle dependency bumps; call out
  majors explicitly. Reusable-workflow/composite-action inputs must stay backward-compatible.

When unsure, defer to [`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md), its `## Maintenance` section, and the path-specific
files under [`.github/instructions/`](instructions/).
